tasks.task: Execute Coverage Tests and Verify Reports
Document Coverage Gaps and Action Items

criterion.title: Coverage Reports Generated
criterion.then: - Coverage reports generate ediliyor - Console'da coverage summary gösteriliyor (text format) - HTML report oluşturuluyor: `coverage/index.html` - LCOV report oluşturuluyor: `coverage/lcov.info` (CI/CD integration için)

criterion.title: Coverage Thresholds Enforced
criterion.then: - Global coverage threshold: %70 (branches, functions, lines, statements) - Utilities coverage threshold: %100 (`./src/common/**/*.ts`) - Services coverage threshold: %80 (`./src/**/services/**/*.ts`) - Threshold'lar karşılanmazsa test suite FAIL ediyor - Threshold violations console'da açıkça gösteriliyor

criterion.title: Coverage Report Formats
criterion.then: - Text format: Console'da summary table - HTML format: Interactive coverage report `coverage/index.html`'de - LCOV format: `coverage/lcov.info` dosyası (CI/CD için)

criterion.title: Coverage Collection Configuration
criterion.then: - `src/**/*.(t|j)s` pattern'i ile tüm source files cover ediliyor - Test files coverage'dan excluded - node_modules/ ve dist/ directories ignored

criterion.title: Coverage Threshold Validation
criterion.then: - Current codebase coverage threshold'ları karşılıyor veya exceeded ediyor - Threshold fail ederse: Hangi threshold'un fail ettiği gösteriliyor - Threshold pass ederse: Exit code 0 (success)

criterion.title: CI/CD Pipeline Integration Ready
criterion.given: LCOV format coverage report generate ediliyor
criterion.then: - `coverage/lcov.info` dosyası CI/CD'de parse edilebilir - Coverage badge generation için data hazır - Codecov/Coveralls integration'a ready

criterion.title: Coverage Report Accessibility
criterion.then: - Interactive UI ile file tree navigable - Her file için detailed coverage view (covered/uncovered lines) - File/folder bazında coverage percentages gösteriliyor

doc.title: Epic Technical Specification: Testing Infrastructure
doc.snippet: 7-step workflow: Test Execution → Instrumentation → Collection → Aggregation → Threshold Check → Report Generation → Exit Code. Istanbul reporter bundles with Jest, generates text/HTML/LCOV formats.

doc.title: Epic Technical Specification: Testing Infrastructure
doc.section: Data Models and Contracts → Test Configuration Schema
doc.snippet: coverageReporters: ['text', 'html', 'lcov'], collectCoverageFrom: ['src/**/*.(t|j)s'], coverageThreshold: global 70%, utilities 100%, services 80%

doc.title: Epic Technical Specification: Testing Infrastructure
doc.snippet: Global threshold 70% (balanced target from PRD NFR-3.1), Utilities 100% (shared code must be well-tested), Services 80% (business logic critical). Enforcement: Tests fail on CI/CD if thresholds not met.

doc.title: Epic Technical Specification: Testing Infrastructure
doc.snippet: Coverage collection overhead: Max 10% slower execution. Coverage report generation: < 3 seconds. Full test suite: < 2 minutes.

doc.title: Testing Strategy
doc.snippet: Jest with Istanbul reporter. Multi-format output: console summary, interactive HTML, machine-readable LCOV. Threshold enforcement prevents quality regression.

doc.title: Story 9.1: Jest Configuration and Test Setup
doc.snippet: Story 9.1 established jest.config.js with coverage configuration. Coverage reporters, thresholds, and collection patterns already configured.

doc.title: Story 9.4: E2E Test Infrastructure
doc.snippet: Test suite baseline: Unit tests (UsersService 12 cases), Integration tests (UsersController), E2E tests (Auth flow 6 cases). Test execution fast: 12 integration tests in 1.262 seconds.

doc.title: Epics Documentation
doc.snippet: Coverage reports generate (`npm run test:cov`). Thresholds: Global 70%, Utilities 100%, Services 80%. Formats: Console summary, HTML (coverage/index.html), LCOV (CI/CD). CI/CD: Coverage threshold fail → build fail.

artifact.reason: Core Jest configuration with coverage settings. Contains coverageReporters, coverageThreshold, collectCoverageFrom patterns. THIS IS THE PRIMARY FILE TO VERIFY for this story.
Configured as ['text', 'html', 'lcov'] - generates all three required report formats
Defines global 70%, utilities 100%, services 80% thresholds. Story validates these thresholds are enforced correctly.
Pattern: ['src/**/*.(t|j)s'] - collects coverage from all source files. Excludes test files automatically.
NPM script: "test:cov": "jest --coverage". This is the command to execute for coverage report generation.
Unit test example (12 test cases from Story 9.2). Contributes to coverage baseline. Well-tested service likely exceeds 80% threshold.
Integration test example (Story 9.3). Tests controller + service + DB. Contributes to overall coverage.
E2E test (Story 9.4). Complete auth flow coverage. 6 test cases covering registration, verification, login, protected routes, refresh, logout.
Common utility test. Contributes to 100% utilities threshold (./src/common/**/*.ts)
Common utility test. Contributes to 100% utilities threshold (./src/common/**/*.ts)
Common utility test. Must meet 100% coverage threshold per jest.config.js
Coverage reports should NOT be committed. Verify coverage/ directory is in .gitignore (Task 6.2)

node.package: Core test framework with bundled Istanbul coverage reporter
NestJS test utilities (TestingModule for unit/integration tests)
HTTP assertion library for E2E tests

constraints.constraint: Story 9.5 is VALIDATION-focused, not configuration creation. Coverage configuration already exists in jest.config.js from Story 9.1. DO NOT create new configuration - VERIFY existing setup works correctly.
Coverage reports are generated artifacts (NOT source code). coverage/ directory must be in .gitignore. CI/CD will generate fresh reports on each build.

Three-tier threshold strategy: - Global: 70% (all metrics: branches, functions, lines, statements) - Utilities (./src/common/**/*.ts): 100% (shared code must be well-tested) - Services (./src/**/services/**/*.ts): 80% (business logic critical)
Coverage collection overhead: Max 10% slower than normal test execution. Coverage report generation: < 3 seconds. Full test suite with coverage: < 2 minutes.

Three required report formats: 1. Text: Console summary (immediate feedback) 2. HTML: Interactive report at coverage/index.html (developer exploration) 3. LCOV: Machine-readable at coverage/lcov.info (CI/CD integration)
Test files automatically excluded from coverage: *.spec.ts, *.integration.spec.ts, *.e2e-spec.ts. Also excluded: node_modules/, dist/. Optional exclusions: main.ts, migrations.
Story 9.5 prepares LCOV format for Epic 11 (CI/CD pipeline). Coverage threshold enforcement will cause build failures in CI/CD if thresholds not met.
If threshold violations exist, DOCUMENT them (do not fail story). This is a validation story - identify gaps, create action plan for future stories to address coverage deficiencies.

interface.name: npm run test:cov
interface.description: Executes Jest with --coverage flag. Generates text, HTML, and LCOV reports. Enforces thresholds (fails if < configured values).

interface.name: coverageReporters
interface.description: Defines output formats for coverage reports. Text (console), HTML (interactive), LCOV (CI/CD).

interface.name: coverageThreshold
interface.description: Enforces minimum coverage percentages. Global 70%, utilities 100%, services 80%. Tests fail if thresholds not met.

interface.name: collectCoverageFrom
interface.description: Glob pattern for files to include in coverage collection. Automatically excludes test files.

interface.name: coverageDirectory
interface.description: Output directory for coverage reports. Generated artifacts: index.html, lcov.info, coverage-final.json

tests.standards: Jest v30.x with Istanbul coverage reporter (bundled). Test pattern: Arrange-Act-Assert (AAA). Coverage collected during test execution via code instrumentation. Reports generated in three formats: text (console summary), HTML (interactive browser UI), LCOV (machine-readable for CI/CD). Threshold enforcement: Tests fail if coverage < configured thresholds (global 70%, utilities 100%, services 80%). Test files automatically excluded from coverage analysis.
tests.locations: Unit tests: src/modules/[module]/__tests__/*.spec.ts Integration tests: src/modules/[module]/__tests__/*.integration.spec.ts E2E tests: test/*.e2e-spec.ts Coverage reports: coverage/ (NOT committed, in .gitignore)

ideas.idea: Test: Execute `npm run test:cov` and verify all three report formats generated: - Assert: Console output contains coverage summary table - Assert: coverage/index.html file exists and is valid HTML - Assert: coverage/lcov.info file exists with valid LCOV format

Test: Run coverage and check current codebase against thresholds: - Parse console output for global coverage percentages - Compare against 70% threshold (branches, functions, lines, statements) - If < 70%, document which files/folders are below threshold - Create action plan for addressing gaps in future stories

Test: Verify threshold enforcement mechanism: - Temporarily modify jest.config.js to lower threshold (e.g., global 99%) - Run `npm run test:cov` and assert exit code is 1 (failure) - Assert console shows which threshold failed - Restore original threshold, verify exit code 0 (success)

Test: Verify coverage collection pattern: - Run coverage and check covered files list - Assert all src/**/*.ts files included (except test files) - Assert test files (*.spec.ts) NOT in coverage report - Assert node_modules/ and dist/ excluded

Test: Verify LCOV format for CI/CD compatibility: - Read coverage/lcov.info file - Parse with LCOV tools (if available) or manual validation - Assert format matches LCOV specification (SF:, FN:, DA: lines) - Confirm ready for Codecov/Coveralls upload in Epic 11

Test: Verify HTML report accessibility: - Open coverage/index.html in browser - Assert file tree navigation works - Click on specific file, assert line-level coverage displayed - Assert color coding: green (covered), red (uncovered), yellow (partial)

Documentation: If threshold violations found, create comprehensive gap analysis: - List top 5 files with lowest coverage - Identify critical paths missing tests (auth, permissions, data access) - Prioritize: Business-critical services > Controllers > Utilities - Create action items for future stories to address gaps
